source_text: The patient should take 500mg of acetaminophen every 4-6 hours for pain. Do not exceed 3000mg daily.
target_text: El paciente debe tomar 500mg de acetaminofeno cada 4-6 horas para el dolor. No exceder 3000mg diarios.

frameworks.name: MQM
frameworks.application: Used MQM error typology for error categorization and dimension scoring

frameworks.name: ISO 17100
frameworks.application: Applied ISO 17100 competence requirements for medical domain evaluation

frameworks.name: DQF
frameworks.application: Applied DQF Life Sciences quality model for accuracy-critical content

evidence: Dosage information accurately preserved (500mg, 4-6 hours, 3000mg daily)
Medical terms generally correct: 'acetaminophen' → 'acetaminofeno' (acceptable in Spain, 'acetaminofén' preferred in Latin America)
Consistent use of medical terminology throughout
Appropriate sentence structure for medical instructions
Appropriate formal register for medical instructions
Correct use of imperative mood for instructions
Number formatting appropriate for Spanish locale
Space between number and unit (500mg → 500 mg would be preferred but 500mg acceptable)
No inconsistent use of terms or phrasing
Appropriate reading level for patient instructions

error_log.description: 'Acetaminofeno' is acceptable in Spain but 'acetaminofén' is the preferred international Spanish term
error_log.suggestion: Consider using 'acetaminofén' or 'paracetamol' for broader locale acceptance

strengths.description: Excellent preservation of critical medical information

strengths.evidence: No instances of inconsistent terminology detected

quality_gates_passed: Every dimension has numeric score and evidence
At least one framework explicitly cited (3 frameworks cited: MQM, ISO 17100, DQF)
Roadmap items have effort, impact, and measurable success metric
Devil's-advocate review completed (challenged accuracy findings, confirmed)

quick_wins.recommendation: Replace 'acetaminofeno' with 'acetaminofén' or 'paracetamol' for broader locale acceptance
Add space between number and unit: '500 mg' instead of '500mg'

major_projects.recommendation: Establish terminology glossary for consistent medication name translations across all documents
major_projects.success_metric: Zero terminology inconsistencies across all medical documents

long_term.recommendation: Implement terminology management system with automated consistency checking
long_term.success_metric: Automated detection of 100% of terminology inconsistencies

long_term.recommendation: Develop locale-specific style guides for medical translations (Spain vs. Latin America)
long_term.success_metric: All future translations aligned with target locale conventions

metadata.confidence_explanation: High confidence based on clear source text, well-formed target translation, and appropriate application of MQM and ISO frameworks
notes: Source text was well-structured medical instruction
